systems.name: TN-S
systems.description: Separate Neutral and Protective Earth conductors throughout the supply
systems.earth_source: DNO transformer star point via separate PE conductor
systems.typical_use_case: Older UK installations, all US installations
systems.notes: In NEC terms this is the standard grounded-system arrangement: neutral grounded at service, EGC bonded to grounded conductor only at service equipment.

systems.name: TN-C-S (PME)
systems.description: Combined Neutral and Earth (PEN) from transformer to service head, then separate within installation
systems.earth_source: DNO PEN conductor — Protective Multiple Earthing
systems.typical_use_case: Default for UK domestic and commercial; PME earth provided by DNO
systems.notes: PME has open-PEN risk; EV chargers, marinas, agricultural require special measures (BS 7671 Section 722, 709, 705).

systems.name: TT
systems.description: No DNO earth — installation provides its own earth electrode
systems.earth_source: Local earth electrode (rod, plate, mat) at the installation
systems.rcd_blanket_requirement_reason: Disconnection time cannot be met by overcurrent alone — Ra is too high. RCD enforces 30/100/300mA fault disconnection per BS 7671 411.5.3
systems.notes: TT requires an RCD on every final circuit (or upstream covering all circuits). Ra target ≤200Ω is the disconnection floor; ≤100Ω is the design target for headroom.

systems.name: IT
systems.description: Source is either isolated from earth or earthed through high impedance; exposed-conductive-parts earthed locally
systems.earth_source: Local earth electrode; source impedance to earth limits first-fault current
common_in: Industrial processes that must not trip on first fault
systems.notes: Out of scope for stage 1 of this skill. Requires Insulation Monitoring Device (IMD).

systems.name: TN-C
systems.description: Combined Neutral+Earth conductor throughout — NO separation within the installation
systems.earth_source: DNO PEN, not split inside installation
systems.permitted_reason: Prohibited in installations covered by BS 7671 and most current EU/IEC codes — RCDs cannot function correctly on a TN-C system
systems.notes: Listed for completeness only. Never specify in a new design.

US.notes: NEC uses different terminology — 'grounded system' ≈ TN-S. NEC 250 requires supplemental grounding electrodes regardless.
